title: Nio reports 30% year-on-year increase in Q1 net loss as expenses rise
text: Net loss reached RMB 6.75 billion (about $930 million) in Q1 2025, a 30.19% year-over-year increase. Adjusted net loss reached RMB 6.28 billion, up 28.1% compared to prior year. Revenue totaled RMB 12.03 billion, falling short of analyst expectations and company guidance range of RMB 12.37-12.86 billion, but represented 21.46% growth year-over-year and down 38.92% sequentially. Automotive revenue reached RMB 9.94 billion, reflecting 18.6% year-over-year increase despite declining 43.1% quarter-over-quarter. Deliveries reached 42,094 vehicles, up 40.07% annually but down 42.09% from Q4 2024, with Nio main brand delivering 27,313 units and Onvo sub-brand contributing 14,781 vehicles. Gross profit improved to RMB 920 million with 7.6% gross margin versus 4.9% prior-year quarter. Vehicle margin reached 10.2%. Company acknowledged operating cash outflow with current liabilities exceeding current assets as of March 31, 2025. Cash reserves totaled RMB 26 billion. Q2 guidance: deliveries 72,000-75,000 units (up 25.5%-30.7% year-over-year); revenue RMB 19.5-20.07 billion (up 11.8%-15.0% annually).
